tasks.title: Project Setup and Input Configuration
tasks.description: Configure Godot 4 project settings, create input action map, and set up the Main.tscn scene router
tasks.details:
1. Open project.godot and configure:
   - Verify config_version=5 (Godot 4.6)
   - Set display/window/size/viewport_width=640 and viewport_height=640
   - Set display/window/size/resizable=true

2. Configure Input Map (Project > Project Settings > Input Map):
   - Create 'move_up' action: bind Arrow Up + W
   - Create 'move_down' action: bind Arrow Down + S  
   - Create 'move_left' action: bind Arrow Left + A
   - Create 'move_right' action: bind Arrow Right + D
   - Create 'pause' action: bind Escape + P
   - Verify ui_accept (Enter/Space) and ui_cancel (Escape) exist by default

3. Create Main.tscn:
   - Root node: Node (rename to 'Main')
   - Attach script Main.gd with scene switching logic:
     ```gdscript
     extends Node
     var current_scene: Node = null
     
     func _ready():
         load_menu()
     
     func load_menu():
         change_scene("res://scenes/MainMenu.tscn")
     
     func load_game():
         change_scene("res://scenes/Game.tscn")
     
     func change_scene(path: String):
         if current_scene:
             current_scene.queue_free()
         var scene = load(path).instantiate()
         add_child(scene)
         current_scene = scene
     ```

4. Create folder structure:
   - res://scenes/ (for .tscn files)
   - res://scripts/ (for .gd files)
   - res://assets/ (for sprites/textures if needed)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Launch project - Main.tscn should be set as main scene in project settings
2. Test input actions work in Input Map tester
3. Verify folder structure exists
4. Confirm Main.gd can compile (no syntax errors)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the project setup into: (1) Configure project.godot display and engine settings, (2) Create and configure input action map with all movement and UI actions, (3) Create folder structure (scenes/, scripts/, assets/), (4) Implement Main.tscn with scene router script and test scene switching logic

tasks.title: Grid Playfield and Static Rendering
tasks.description: Implement the 20x20 grid playfield with visible bounds, render static snake and apple at fixed positions
tasks.details:
1. Create Playfield.gd script:
   ```gdscript
   extends Node2D
   const GRID_WIDTH = 20
   const GRID_HEIGHT = 20  
   const CELL_SIZE = 32
   
   func _ready():
       queue_redraw()
   
   func _draw():
       # Draw background
       draw_rect(Rect2(0, 0, GRID_WIDTH * CELL_SIZE, GRID_HEIGHT * CELL_SIZE), Color.BLACK, true)
       # Draw grid lines (optional, for visibility)
       for x in range(GRID_WIDTH + 1):
           draw_line(Vector2(x * CELL_SIZE, 0), Vector2(x * CELL_SIZE, GRID_HEIGHT * CELL_SIZE), Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2))
       for y in range(GRID_HEIGHT + 1):
           draw_line(Vector2(0, y * CELL_SIZE), Vector2(GRID_WIDTH * CELL_SIZE, y * CELL_SIZE), Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2))
       # Draw border
       draw_rect(Rect2(0, 0, GRID_WIDTH * CELL_SIZE, GRID_HEIGHT * CELL_SIZE), Color.WHITE, false, 2.0)
   ```

2. Create Game.tscn:
   - Root: Node2D (rename to 'Game')
   - Add child Node2D named 'Playfield' with Playfield.gd script
   - Add Camera2D centered at (GRID_WIDTH * CELL_SIZE / 2, GRID_HEIGHT * CELL_SIZE / 2)

3. Create Snake.gd (static for now):
   ```gdscript
   extends Node2D
   const CELL_SIZE = 32
   var body: Array[Vector2i] = [Vector2i(10, 10), Vector2i(9, 10), Vector2i(8, 10)] # Head at index 0
   
   func _draw():
       for i in body.size():
           var cell = body[i]
           var color = Color.GREEN if i == 0 else Color.LIME_GREEN # Head darker
           draw_rect(Rect2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE, cell.y * CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE), color, true)
   ```

4. Create Apple.gd (static):
   ```gdscript
   extends Node2D
   const CELL_SIZE = 32
   var position_grid: Vector2i = Vector2i(15, 10)
   
   func _draw():
       draw_rect(Rect2(position_grid.x * CELL_SIZE, position_grid.y * CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE), Color.RED, true)
   ```

5. Add Snake and Apple nodes to Game.tscn as children of Game root
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run Game.tscn - should see 20x20 black grid with white border
2. Verify static snake (3 green cells) appears at grid position (8-10, 10)
3. Verify static red apple appears at grid position (15, 10)
4. Check camera framing - entire grid should be visible
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide static rendering into: (1) Create Playfield.gd with grid constants and _draw() method for background and grid lines, (2) Create Game.tscn with Node2D root and Playfield child node, (3) Implement Snake.gd with static body array and _draw() rendering, (4) Implement Apple.gd with static position and _draw() rendering, (5) Configure Camera2D positioning and test complete scene rendering

tasks.title: Tick-Driven Snake Movement
tasks.description: Implement Timer-based game tick system and make snake move continuously in a fixed direction
tasks.details:
1. Update Snake.gd to add movement:
   ```gdscript
   extends Node2D
   const CELL_SIZE = 32
   var body: Array[Vector2i] = [Vector2i(10, 10), Vector2i(9, 10), Vector2i(8, 10)]
   var heading: Vector2i = Vector2i.RIGHT
   
   func _draw():
       for i in body.size():
           var cell = body[i]
           var color = Color.GREEN if i == 0 else Color.LIME_GREEN
           draw_rect(Rect2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE, cell.y * CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE), color, true)
   
   func move():
       var new_head = body[0] + heading
       body.insert(0, new_head)
       body.pop_back() # Remove tail (no growth yet)
       queue_redraw()
   ```

2. Update Game.tscn and add Game.gd script:
   - Add Timer node as child of Game root, name it 'GameTick'
   - Set Timer properties: wait_time=0.15, autostart=true, one_shot=false
   
3. Create Game.gd:
   ```gdscript
   extends Node2D
   @onready var snake = $Snake
   @onready var game_tick = $GameTick
   
   func _ready():
       game_tick.timeout.connect(_on_game_tick)
   
   func _on_game_tick():
       snake.move()
   ```

4. Ensure Snake node has access to move() signal
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run Game.tscn - snake should move right continuously
2. Verify movement happens at ~150ms intervals (tick rate)
3. Snake should maintain 3-cell length as it moves
4. Snake should eventually move off-screen right (no collision detection yet)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break movement system into: (1) Add Timer node to Game.tscn and configure tick rate properties, (2) Update Snake.gd to add heading vector and move() method with body manipulation, (3) Implement Game.gd signal connection between Timer.timeout and snake movement, (4) Test tick-based movement and verify snake maintains constant length while moving

tasks.title: Player Input and Heading Control
tasks.description: Wire keyboard input to change snake heading with queued application and 180-degree reversal rejection
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run game and press arrow keys / WASD - snake should change direction
2. Test 180° reversal rejection: moving right, press left - should be ignored
3. Test rapid input between ticks: right→down→left should result in down (not illegal left)
4. Verify snake responds on next tick after input, not immediately
5. Test all four directions work correctly
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide input system into: (1) Add input queueing variables (heading, queued_heading) to Snake.gd, (2) Implement queue_direction() method with 180-degree reversal rejection logic, (3) Update Snake.move() to apply queued heading at tick boundaries, (4) Add _process() input polling in Game.gd for all four directions, (5) Test edge cases: 180-degree reversal blocking, rapid multi-input between ticks, and input-tick synchronization

tasks.title: Apple Eating and Snake Growth
tasks.description: Implement collision detection between snake head and apple, grow snake on eat, respawn apple in empty cell
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run game and navigate snake to apple
2. When snake head touches apple, snake should grow by 1 segment
3. Apple should immediately respawn in a random empty cell
4. Verify apple never spawns on snake body
5. Test multiple apple eats - snake should keep growing
6. Verify growth happens at tail (new segment stays behind)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break growth mechanics into: (1) Implement Apple.respawn() method with empty cell calculation logic avoiding snake body, (2) Add pending_growth counter and grow() method to Snake.gd, (3) Update Snake.move() to conditionally skip tail removal based on pending_growth, (4) Add collision detection in Game._on_game_tick() to check head position against apple, (5) Test growth behavior, apple respawn randomness, and edge case where grid is nearly full

tasks.title: Collision Detection and Game Over State
tasks.description: Implement wall and self-collision detection, trigger game over state, add basic restart mechanism
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run game and deliberately hit top wall - game should stop, print message
2. Hit bottom, left, right walls - all should trigger game over
3. Grow snake long enough to make it collide with itself - should trigger game over
4. Press Space or Enter during game over - scene should reload and restart
5. Verify snake stops moving after game over
6. Verify input is ignored during game over state
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide collision system into: (1) Implement Snake.check_collision() with wall boundary checking using grid constants, (2) Add self-collision detection looping through body segments, (3) Add is_game_over state flag and game_over() method to Game.gd, (4) Update _on_game_tick() to check collisions before apple eating, (5) Implement restart_game() using get_tree().reload_current_scene(), (6) Update _process() to handle game over input blocking and restart input detection

tasks.title: Score HUD and Score Tracking
tasks.description: Add score counter that increments on apple eat and display it via CanvasLayer HUD
tasks.details:
1. Update Game.gd to track score:
   ```gdscript
   var score: int = 0
   @onready var hud = $HUD
   
   func _ready():
       game_tick.timeout.connect(_on_game_tick)
       update_hud()
   
   func _on_game_tick():
       if is_game_over:
           return
       
       snake.move()
       
       if snake.check_collision():
           game_over()
           return
       
       if snake.body[0] == apple.position_grid:
           snake.grow()
           apple.respawn(snake.body)
           score += 1
           update_hud()
   
   func update_hud():
       hud.get_node("ScoreLabel").text = "Score: " + str(score)
   ```

2. Create HUD scene in Game.tscn:
   - Add CanvasLayer node as child of Game, name it 'HUD'
   - Add Label child to HUD, name it 'ScoreLabel'
   - Set ScoreLabel properties:
     - Position: (10, 10) top-left
     - Text: "Score: 0"
     - Font size: 24
     - Modulate color: White
     - Add Theme Override for font size if needed

3. Style the label for visibility against dark background
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run game - "Score: 0" should appear in top-left corner
2. Eat first apple - score should update to "Score: 1"
3. Eat multiple apples - score should increment correctly
4. Trigger game over - score should remain visible showing final score
5. Restart - score should reset to 0
6. Verify label is readable against black grid background
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break HUD implementation into: (1) Add CanvasLayer and Label nodes to Game.tscn scene tree, (2) Configure ScoreLabel properties (position, font size, color theming), (3) Add score variable and update_hud() method to Game.gd, (4) Integrate score increment in apple eating logic and test HUD updates during gameplay and restart

tasks.title: Persistent High Score System
tasks.description: Implement HighScore autoload singleton to load, track, and save high score to user:// filesystem
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run game, get score of 5, trigger game over
2. Check user data directory (OS-specific, use OS.get_user_data_dir()) - highscore.save should exist with value 5
3. Restart game, get score of 3, game over - high score should remain 5
4. Get score of 10, game over - highscore.save should update to 10
5. Close and relaunch entire Godot project - high score should persist
6. Manually delete highscore.save - game should handle gracefully, start at 0
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide persistence system into: (1) Create HighScore.gd autoload singleton script with high_score variable and SAVE_PATH constant, (2) Implement load_high_score() using FileAccess API with error handling for missing files, (3) Implement save_high_score() with comparison logic and file writing, (4) Register HighScore as autoload in project settings, (5) Integrate HighScore.save_high_score() into Game.game_over() and test persistence across launches

tasks.title: Main Menu Scene
tasks.description: Create main menu with title, high score display, Start button, and Quit button
tasks.details:
1. Create MainMenu.tscn:
   - Root: Control node (rename to 'MainMenu')
   - Add VBoxContainer centered on screen (anchor preset: Center)
   - Children of VBoxContainer:
     a. Label 'TitleLabel' - text: "SNAKE", font size: 48, alignment: center
     b. Label 'HighScoreLabel' - text: "High Score: 0", font size: 24, alignment: center
     c. Button 'StartButton' - text: "Start Game"
     d. Button 'QuitButton' - text: "Quit"
   - Set VBoxContainer separation: 20

2. Create MainMenu.gd script:
   ```gdscript
   extends Control
   @onready var high_score_label = $VBoxContainer/HighScoreLabel
   @onready var start_button = $VBoxContainer/StartButton
   @onready var quit_button = $VBoxContainer/QuitButton
   
   func _ready():
       high_score_label.text = "High Score: " + str(HighScore.get_high_score())
       start_button.pressed.connect(_on_start_pressed)
       quit_button.pressed.connect(_on_quit_pressed)
       start_button.grab_focus() # Keyboard navigation
   
   func _on_start_pressed():
       get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/Game.tscn")
   
   func _on_quit_pressed():
       get_tree().quit()
   ```

3. Update Main.gd to start with menu:
   ```gdscript
   func _ready():
       get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/MainMenu.tscn")
   ```

4. Set Main.tscn as main scene in Project Settings
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Launch game - should open to Main Menu, not Game scene
2. Verify high score displays correctly (should match saved value)
3. Click Start or press Enter - should transition to Game.tscn
4. Return to menu after game over - high score should update if beaten
5. Click Quit or press Escape then Enter - application should close
6. Test keyboard navigation (Tab to cycle, Enter to select)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break menu creation into: (1) Create MainMenu.tscn with Control root and VBoxContainer layout structure, (2) Add and configure all UI elements (title label, high score label, buttons) with theme properties, (3) Implement MainMenu.gd with _ready() to display HighScore data and setup button signals, (4) Implement scene transition methods (_on_start_pressed, _on_quit_pressed) using scene tree API, (5) Test menu flow: launch to menu, start game, game over, return to menu with updated high score

tasks.title: Game Over Screen and Pause Overlay
tasks.description: Create game over overlay showing final score, high score, restart/menu options, and implement pause system
tasks.details:
1. Create GameOver UI in Game.tscn:
   - Add CanvasLayer named 'GameOverOverlay', set visible=false initially
   - Add ColorRect child filling screen with semi-transparent black (Color: 0, 0, 0, 0.7)
   - Add VBoxContainer centered with:
     a. Label 'GameOverLabel' - text: "GAME OVER", font size: 48
     b. Label 'FinalScoreLabel' - text: "Score: 0", font size: 32
     c. Label 'HighScoreLabel' - text: "High Score: 0", font size: 24
     d. Label 'NewBestLabel' - text: "NEW BEST!", font size: 28, color: yellow, initially hidden
     e. Button 'RestartButton' - text: "Restart"
     f. Button 'MenuButton' - text: "Return to Menu"

2. Create PauseOverlay in Game.tscn:
   - Add CanvasLayer named 'PauseOverlay', set visible=false, process_mode=PROCESS_MODE_WHEN_PAUSED
   - Add ColorRect with semi-transparent background
   - Add centered VBoxContainer with:
     a. Label - text: "PAUSED", font size: 48
     b. Label - text: "Press ESC or P to Resume", font size: 20

3. Update Game.gd:
   ```gdscript
   var is_paused: bool = false
   @onready var game_over_overlay = $GameOverOverlay
   @onready var pause_overlay = $PauseOverlay
   
   func _process(_delta):
       if is_game_over:
           return
       
       if Input.is_action_just_pressed("pause"):
           toggle_pause()
           return
       
       if is_paused:
           return
       
       # Input handling...
   
   func toggle_pause():
       is_paused = !is_paused
       pause_overlay.visible = is_paused
       get_tree().paused = is_paused
   
   func game_over():
       is_game_over = true
       game_tick.stop()
       var old_high = HighScore.get_high_score()
       HighScore.save_high_score(score)
       var new_high = HighScore.get_high_score()
       show_game_over_screen(old_high, new_high)
   
   func show_game_over_screen(old_high: int, new_high: int):
       game_over_overlay.get_node("VBoxContainer/FinalScoreLabel").text = "Score: " + str(score)
       game_over_overlay.get_node("VBoxContainer/HighScoreLabel").text = "High Score: " + str(new_high)
       game_over_overlay.get_node("VBoxContainer/NewBestLabel").visible = (new_high > old_high)
       game_over_overlay.get_node("VBoxContainer/RestartButton").pressed.connect(restart_game)
       game_over_overlay.get_node("VBoxContainer/MenuButton").pressed.connect(return_to_menu)
       game_over_overlay.visible = true
   
   func restart_game():
       get_tree().paused = false
       get_tree().reload_current_scene()
   
   func return_to_menu():
       get_tree().paused = false
       get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/MainMenu.tscn")
   ```

4. Set process_mode for game nodes:
   - Snake, Apple, Playfield, GameTick, HUD: PROCESS_MODE_PAUSABLE
   - PauseOverlay: PROCESS_MODE_WHEN_PAUSED
tasks.testStrategy:
1. During gameplay, press Esc or P - pause overlay should appear, game should freeze
2. Press Esc/P again - game should resume
3. Trigger game over - overlay should show with correct score
4. If new high score, verify "NEW BEST!" appears in yellow
5. If not new high score, verify "NEW BEST!" is hidden
6. Click Restart - should reload game scene and start fresh
7. Click Return to Menu - should go back to main menu with updated high score
8. Verify input is blocked while paused (snake doesn't queue moves)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide overlay systems into: (1) Create GameOverOverlay CanvasLayer with semi-transparent ColorRect and VBoxContainer in Game.tscn, (2) Add all game over UI elements including conditional 'NEW BEST!' label, (3) Create PauseOverlay CanvasLayer with PROCESS_MODE_WHEN_PAUSED and pause UI, (4) Implement toggle_pause() using get_tree().paused and overlay visibility, (5) Enhance game_over() to compare old/new high scores and show_game_over_screen(), (6) Set process_mode properties on all game nodes for proper pause behavior, (7) Test pause during gameplay, game over states (new high score vs not), and navigation between overlays/scenes

tasks.title: Visual Polish and Refinement
tasks.description: Polish visual presentation: distinct head sprite, consistent theming, proper HUD positioning, readable colors
tasks.details:
1. Update Snake.gd rendering for visual distinction:
   ```gdscript
   func _draw():
       for i in body.size():
           var cell = body[i]
           var rect = Rect2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE + 1, cell.y * CELL_SIZE + 1, CELL_SIZE - 2, CELL_SIZE - 2) # 1px padding
           
           if i == 0: # Head
               draw_rect(rect, Color.DARK_GREEN, true)
               # Draw eyes
               var eye_size = 4
               var eye_offset = 8
               if heading == Vector2i.RIGHT:
                   draw_circle(Vector2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE + CELL_SIZE - eye_offset, cell.y * CELL_SIZE + 10), eye_size, Color.WHITE)
                   draw_circle(Vector2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE + CELL_SIZE - eye_offset, cell.y * CELL_SIZE + 22), eye_size, Color.WHITE)
               # Add similar for other directions
           else: # Body
               draw_rect(rect, Color.LIME_GREEN, true)
   ```

2. Refine color scheme:
   - Background: Color(0.1, 0.1, 0.1) (dark gray instead of pure black)
   - Grid lines: Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2)
   - Border: Color.WHITE, 3px thickness
   - Apple: Color(0.9, 0.2, 0.2) (bright red)
   - Snake head: Color(0.2, 0.6, 0.2) (darker green)
   - Snake body: Color(0.4, 0.8, 0.4) (lighter green)

3. Update all UI text styling for consistency:
   - Use same font family throughout
   - Ensure all labels have proper contrast
   - Add subtle shadows/outlines to improve readability

4. Polish HUD positioning:
   - Score label: top-left (16, 16) with padding
   - Add background panel to HUD for better readability

5. Ensure all overlays use consistent styling:
   - Same semi-transparent background color
   - Same font sizes for equivalent elements
   - Consistent button styling
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Visual inspection - snake head should be clearly distinguishable from body
2. Verify eyes on snake head point in direction of movement
3. Check all colors are readable and have good contrast
4. Verify 1-2px padding between grid cells makes segments distinct
5. Test all UI screens (menu, game, pause, game over) use consistent theme
6. Verify HUD doesn't overlap with playfield content
7. Take screenshots and verify professional appearance
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break polish into: (1) Update Snake._draw() to add distinct head rendering with darker color and 1px padding, (2) Implement directional eye rendering on snake head based on heading vector, (3) Refine Playfield color scheme (background, grid lines, border) for better aesthetics, (4) Apply consistent color palette to Apple and Snake body segments, (5) Create unified theme/style for all UI text elements (font, size, shadows/outlines), (6) Add HUD background panel and ensure consistent styling across all overlays (menu, pause, game over)

tasks.title: Export Configuration and Build Verification
tasks.description: Configure Windows export preset, create executable build, and verify persistence works in exported build
tasks.details:
1. Configure Windows export preset:
   - Project > Export > Add > Windows Desktop
   - Set export path: builds/windows/Snake.exe
   - Application settings:
     - Name: "Snake"
     - Icon: Use icon.svg or create custom 256x256 icon
   - Verify 'Embed PCK' is enabled
   - Set architecture to x86_64

2. Export the project:
   - Click 'Export Project'
   - Choose output directory: builds/windows/
   - Export with debug disabled for final build

3. Test exported build:
   - Launch Snake.exe directly (not from Godot)
   - Play full game loop: Menu > Game > Eat apples > Game Over > Restart > Return to Menu
   - Close exe completely
   - Relaunch Snake.exe
   - Verify high score persisted between launches
   - Check OS.get_user_data_dir() for save location

4. Create distribution folder structure:
   ```
   Snake_v1.0/
   ├── Snake.exe
   ├── README.txt (optional: controls, credits)
   └── (any .pck files if not embedded)
   ```

5. Document export settings in project notes for future builds
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Export completes without errors
2. Launch Snake.exe - game runs identically to editor version
3. All input actions work (arrows, WASD, Esc, P, Enter, Space)
4. Play complete game, achieve score of 10, quit
5. Relaunch exe - high score should show 10 on main menu
6. Verify highscore.save file exists in user data directory
7. Test on clean Windows machine (no Godot installed) to verify independence
8. File size should be reasonable (<50MB for simple 2D game)
9. No console window appears (unless debug build)
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide export process into: (1) Configure Windows Desktop export preset with all settings (name, icon, embed PCK, architecture), (2) Create builds/windows/ directory structure and export executable, (3) Test exported build on development machine with full game loop and persistence verification, (4) Verify user data directory location and highscore.save file creation in exported build, (5) Document export settings and test on clean Windows machine without Godot to ensure independence
